children.text: Chapter One: The Onset of Contention
children.label: section_header

children.text: Contentious episodes begin not with the loudest grievance but with the first instance in which previously isolated complaints are recognized by their bearers as instances of a shared condition.
children.label: text

children.text: This recognition is the true onset, prior to any organized mobilization, and it is the analytic starting point this chapter develops across three illustrative episodes.
children.label: text

children.text: 1.1 Grievance Recognition and Framing
children.label: section_header

children.text: A brief methodological note on how grievances are coded for this study.
children.label: text

children.text: lalrodac:lioo
children.label: section_header

children.text: Chapter Two: Escalation Dynamics
children.label: section_header

children.text: Once a shared grievance is recognized, escalation follows a predictable sequence: informal gatherings widen into semi-formal assemblies, and that test either provokes repression or further widens participation.
children.label: text

children.text: This chapter traces that sequence across the same three episodes introduced earlier, comparing the pace of escalation across cases.
children.label: text

children.text: 2.1 Informal Gatherings to Semi-Formal Assemblies
children.label: section_header

children.text: A short note distinguishing informal gatherings from semi-formal assemblies for coding purposes.
children.label: text

children.text: A successful program does all of them at once.
children.label: section_header

children.text: Chapter Three: Settlement and Aftermath
children.label: section_header

children.text: Every episode surveyed here eventually settles, whether through negotiated concession, exhaustion, or suppression, and the settlement terms shape whether the grievance resurfaces.
children.label: text

children.text: This closing chapter compares settlement terms across the three episodes and draws out their shared aftermath dynamics.
children.label: text

children.text: 3.1 Negotiated Concession Pathways
children.label: section_header

children.text: A brief typology of concession pathways observed across the surveyed episodes.
children.label: text

children.text: Chapter Four: Comparative Synthesis
children.label: section_header

children.text: Drawing the three preceding chapters together, this closing chapter compares the onset, escalation, and settlement patterns across every episode surveyed.
children.label: text

children.text: The comparison yields a small set of recurring sequences that recur regardless of the episode is own local context.
children.label: text

children.text: 4.1 Cross-Case Convergence Patterns
children.label: section_header

children.text: A short note on the convergence measure used to compare cases in this section.
children.label: text
